commit.message:
Initial commit: .NET remote Whisper transcription server

Minimal ASP.NET Web API (net10.0) that provides remote Whisper AI
transcription for OpenScanner. Shells out to whisper-cli from whisper.cpp.

Endpoints:
- POST /transcribe: accepts multipart WAV upload + optional prompt,
  returns JSON with transcribed text
- GET /health: returns server status and model info

Features:
- Configurable whisper binary, model path, and timeout via appsettings.json
- Serialized transcription requests to avoid overloading
- Radio-context default prompt matching OpenScanner local service
- Blank audio detection and filtering

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: orchestrator dispatch uses wrong priority label names (#708)

* Initial plan

* fix: use correct priority labels (P1:high/P2:medium) in orchestrator dispatch and add stuck-issue re-dispatch

The orchestrator's backlog dispatch queried for labels 'P1' and 'P2', but
the repository uses 'P1:high' and 'P2:medium'. This meant the API call
returned zero issues every cycle, preventing any automatic dispatch.

Also adds stuck re-dispatch: if an issue is assigned to copilot[bot] for
>30 minutes with no open PR, the orchestrator removes and re-adds the
assignee to re-trigger the Copilot coding-agent webhook.

Closes #698

commit.message:
Fix improper indent on West Workspace and Project Config View (#433)

* Initial plan

* fix: replace path separators in tree item IDs to prevent incorrect indentation

VS Code's internal tree handle construction uses `/` as a hierarchy
separator. When TreeItem IDs contain `/` or `\` (from file paths or
as deliberate separators), VS Code infers phantom parent-child
relationships, causing incorrect indentation in tree views.

- WestWorkspaceView: replace both `/` and `\` in installPath IDs
- ProjectConfigView: replace both `/` and `\` in filename IDs
- ProjectTreeView: replace `/` separator with `.` in all IDs

commit.message:
Fix channel routing: keep all effects in global chain

WASM engines (LibopenmptEngine, FurnaceDispatch, SunVox, SID, etc.) bypass
channelOutputs entirely — audio goes directly from worklet to masterInput/synthBus.
The mixer creates channelOutput entries for volume/pan/mute control, but no
signal flows through them. The previous parallel send approach connected to
these empty shells, removing the effect from the global chain and placing it
in a dead-end send — causing the effect to disappear.

Fix: All effects always go through the global master effects chain regardless
of selectedChannels. The channel preference is stored in the config for future
use when proper per-channel separation is implemented. The broken parallel
send architecture is bypassed (teardown called, no new sends created).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix channel routing: fall back to global chain when channelOutputs unavailable

WASM engines (LibopenmptEngine, FurnaceDispatch, SunVox, SID, etc.) bypass
the per-channel channelOutputs system — their audio goes directly from the
WASM worklet to masterInput. When a user routed an effect to specific channels,
the effect was removed from the global chain but couldn't connect to any
channel outputs, resulting in the effect being in NEITHER chain (no effect).

Fix: ChannelRoutedEffects.rebuild() now pre-checks whether channel outputs
actually exist. If no channels have audio nodes (WASM playback), the effect
ID is NOT returned in the successfully-routed list. ToneEngine.rebuildMasterEffects()
then falls those effects back into the global chain so they still process audio.

This means:
- During WASM module playback: channel selection is stored but effect stays global
- During native synth playback: channel routing works via parallel sends
- No more silent/missing effects when clicking channel buttons

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add SF2 live editing engine with C64 memory bridge

SF2Engine wraps C64SIDEngine (websid backend) providing:
- readRAM/writeRAM on ScriptNodePlayerEngine → C64SIDEngine → SF2Engine
- High-level edit ops: writeInstrumentByte, writeCommandByte,
  writeTableByte, writeSequence, writeOrderEntry
- Sequence packer/unpacker: SF2SeqEvent[] ↔ packed C64 memory format
- Flight recorder: captures 25 SID registers per rAF frame (2048 buffer)
- SID register reading: readSIDRegisters, readSIDVoice

Integration:
- NativeEngineRouting detects sf2StoreData on song, creates SF2Engine
  with driver info (memory map, table defs) for live editing
- TrackerReplayer stores SF2Engine ref, getSF2Engine() accessor
- useSF2Engine hook: acquires engine ref, polls SID regs at 20Hz
- useSF2LiveSync hook: subscribes to store edits (instruments, orders,
  sequences) and pushes changes to C64 RAM immediately
- Both SF2View (DOM) and PixiSF2View (GL) activate live sync

Architecture matches native SF2 editor 1:1:
  User edit → store → writeRAM(addr, val) → driver reads next frame → SID plays

_show_error_dialog executed action callbacks synchronously while
_is_processing was still True, causing the re-entrancy guard to block
retry actions. Using QTimer.singleShot(0) defers execution until after
the finally block resets _is_processing.
